 {"id":"gt-um4iu","title":"Enhance gt status with role hierarchy view","description":"Show identity and role hierarchy in gt status:

## Current Output
Flat list of agents by rig, no clear role hierarchy.

## Desired Output
Tree structure showing:
- Town-level roles (Mayor, Deacon)
- Per-rig role slots with their role_bead references
- Each agent slot with pinned work

Example:
```
Town: gt
├── 🎩 Mayor (gt-mayor-role)
│   └── gt-mayor [running] → gt-u7dxq
├── 🔔 Deacon
│   └── gt-deacon [running]
└── Rigs
    └── gastown/
        ├── 👁 Witness (gt-witness-role)
        │   └── gt-witness-gastown [running]
        ├── 🏭 Refinery (gt-refinery-role)
        │   └── gt-refinery-gastown [running] → (work)
        ├── 👷 Crew
        │   ├── jack [running] → gt-xxxx
        │   └── joe [running]
        └── 😺 Polecats
            └── (none)
```

## Key Changes
1. Use tree-style indentation (├── └──)
2. Group agents by role type
3. Show role_bead for each role category
4. Use emojis for quick visual scanning","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:32:55.007132-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-28T19:35:55.198677-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T19:35:55.198677-08:00"}

## Docs to update

## New section content

### Molecule Navigation

Finding your place:
  bd mol current              # Where am I?
  bd mol current gt-abc       # Status of specific molecule

Seamless transitions:
  bd close gt-abc.3 --continue   # Close and advance
  bd close gt-abc.3 --no-auto    # Close but don't auto-claim next

The old way (3 commands):
  bd close gt-abc.3
  bd ready --parent=gt-abc
  bd update gt-abc.4 --status=in_progress

The new way (1 command):
  bd close gt-abc.3 --continue

 {"id":"gt-ztvjn","title":"Digest: mol-deacon-patrol","description":"Patrol 20: All healthy - handoff threshold reached","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T13:35:01.827415-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T15:52:58.447894-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T15:52:58.447894-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"gt-zut3","title":"Immediate daemon notification on lifecycle request","description":"When gt handoff sends a lifecycle request to the daemon (via bd mail send deacon/), the daemon only discovers it on its next heartbeat poll (every 5 min). Workers wait unnecessarily for retirement.

## Current Behavior
1. Worker runs gt handoff
2. Lifecycle mail sent to deacon/ via beads
3. Worker blocks waiting for retirement
4. Daemon heartbeat runs (up to 5 min later)
5. Daemon processes lifecycle request and kills session

## Expected Behavior
Lifecycle requests should be processed immediately, not on poll interval.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A: SIGUSR1 Signal (simplest)
1. Add SIGUSR1 handler in daemon that calls ProcessLifecycleRequests()
2. gt handoff reads daemon.pid and sends SIGUSR1 after mail send

### Option B: Unix Socket
1. Daemon listens on ~/gt/daemon/lifecycle.sock
2. gt handoff connects and sends 'process' message

### Option C: Watchfile + fsnotify
1. gt handoff touches ~/gt/daemon/lifecycle.trigger
2. Daemon uses fsnotify to detect and process

Recommend Option A for simplicity.

## Affected Files
- internal/daemon/daemon.go - add signal handler
- internal/cmd/handoff.go - send signal after mail","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:20:07.616335-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.627184-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.627184-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"feature"}

+{"id":"gt-zvte2","title":"Standardize agent bead naming: prefix-rig-role-name","description":"## The Problem

Agent bead IDs use INCONSISTENT naming patterns:

| Current (WRONG) | Should Be (prefix-rig-role-name) |
|-----------------|----------------------------------|
| gt-witness-gastown | gt-gastown-witness |
| gt-refinery-gastown | gt-gastown-refinery |
| gt-crew-gastown-max | gt-gastown-crew-max |
| gt-polecat-gastown-Toast | gt-gastown-polecat-Toast |

Town-level agents (no rig) are OK:
- gt-mayor ✓
- gt-deacon ✓

## The Convention

```
prefix-rig-role-name

Where:
- prefix: always 'gt'
- rig: rig name (absent for town-level agents)
- role: witness|refinery|crew|polecat|mayor|deacon
- name: agent name (absent for singleton roles like witness/refinery)
```

Examples:
- `gt-gastown-witness` (rig=gastown, role=witness, no name)
- `gt-gastown-crew-max` (rig=gastown, role=crew, name=max)
- `gt-beads-polecat-Toast` (rig=beads, role=polecat, name=Toast)
- `gt-mayor` (no rig, role=mayor, no name)

## Files to Update

### Agent bead ID generation
- internal/cmd/status.go (lines 316-335)
- internal/daemon/lifecycle.go (lines 546-579)
- internal/cmd/crew_add.go
- internal/polecat/manager.go
- internal/rig/manager.go
- internal/doctor/agent_beads_check.go
- internal/cmd/prime.go
- internal/session/names.go (session names vs bead IDs)

### Commands to verify
- gt status - displays agent beads
- gt doctor - creates missing agent beads
- gt rig add - creates witness/refinery beads
- gt crew add - creates crew beads
- gt sling - routes by agent ID
- gt mol status - looks up agent by ID

## Migration

1. Update all ID generation code
2. Create new agent beads with correct IDs
3. Migrate hook/role slots to new beads
4. Update any mail addresses
5. Delete old incorrectly-named beads

## Also from this session

Role bead slot cleanup was done - CreateAgentBead now calls bd slot set.
But legacy description parsing still exists in daemon/lifecycle.go.

## Reference
- ~/gt/docs/agent-as-bead.md
- Session 2025-12-29: Fixed role bead patterns (gt-crew-role etc)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T14:10:33.310182-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/max","updated_at":"2025-12-29T14:10:33.310182-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-zx3","title":"Per-rig beads repo configuration","description":"Add per-rig beads configuration to rig config schema.

## Config Schema

In each rig's config.json:

```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "name": "wyvern",
  "git_url": "https://github.com/steveyegge/wyvern",
  "beads": {
    "repo": "local",    // "local" | "<path>" | "<git-url>"
    "root": null,       // Override bd --root (optional)
    "prefix": "wyv"     // Issue prefix for this rig
  }
}
```

## Repo Options

| Value | Meaning | Use Case |
|-------|---------|----------|
| `"local"` | Use project's `.beads/` | Own projects, full commit access |
| `"<path>"` | Use beads at path | OSS contributions |
| `"<git-url>"` | Clone and use repo | Team shared beads |

## Environment Injection

When spawning polecats, Gas Town sets:
```bash
export BEADS_ROOT="<resolved-path>"
```

## Resolution Logic

```go
func ResolveBeadsRoot(rigConfig *RigConfig, rigPath string) (string, error) {
    beads := rigConfig.Beads
    switch {
    case beads.Root != "":
        return beads.Root, nil
    case beads.Repo == "local" || beads.Repo == "":
        return filepath.Join(rigPath, ".beads"), nil
    case strings.HasPrefix(beads.Repo, "/"):
        return beads.Repo, nil
    case strings.Contains(beads.Repo, "://"):
        return cloneAndResolve(beads.Repo)
    default:
        return filepath.Join(rigPath, beads.Repo), nil
    }
}
```

## Backwards Compatibility

If `beads` section missing, assume `"repo": "local"`.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:16.660049-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:54.627354-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-zx3","depends_on_id":"gt-l3c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:35.726502-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:54.627354-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
